question: How does Algolia handle multi-brand search with personalized results across separate catalogs?

panels.label: single · neural
panels.answer: You can manage all your brands under a single Algolia account, keeping a separate index per brand so each catalog stays distinct while sharing one platform. You can then set a different custom ranking per brand — for example a luxury brand can rank high-margin items first while an outlet brand ranks sale items first. For shared learnings, Algolia's Personalization builds a single user profile across your domains, so a shopper's behavior on one brand site can inform the results they see on another, improving relevance and conversion across the portfolio.

sources.title: Custom ranking of results per category
sources.text: Adjust custom rankings per category so results can be ordered differently for each part of your catalog.

sources.title: Leveling up search with AI-enabled personalization
sources.text: This eBook is a primer on AI-enabled personalization for ecommerce — what AI personalization is, how and where to use it, and why it is a worthwhile investment. Personalization can build a single user profile from behavior across sites.

sources.title: Personalized catalogs
sources.text: Maintain separate indices per brand so each brand website keeps a distinct catalog under one account.

panels.generatedFollowUp: Are your six brands B2C retail, B2B wholesale, or a mix — so I can tailor the personalization and ranking setup?
